items.headline: MiSTer Companion Is Now Available on macOS
items.message:
MiSTer Companion is now available for macOS.

This brings the desktop version of MiSTer Companion to Apple Silicon Macs. The macOS version is still unsigned, so the first launch may require manually allowing the app through macOS Privacy & Security settings.

Thank you to everyone who helped test the macOS build.

items.headline: MC-Updater Is Available for Windows and Linux
items.message:
MC-Updater is now available for Windows and Linux.

MC-Updater can be installed directly from MiSTer Companion by opening the settings icon in the app. Once installed, it can help update MiSTer Companion more easily on supported platforms.

macOS support for MC-Updater is planned to follow in the near future.

items.headline: MiSTer Companion for Android Is Now on Google Play
items.message:
MiSTer Companion for Android is now available on Google Play.

The Android app is a great way to support my work and use MiSTer Companion on the go. Think of it as MiSTer Companion Lite: a more focused Android version of the desktop app. Some desktop features are not possible on mobile because of platform limitations, while others may be added in future updates.

A huge thank you to everyone who helped beta test the Android app. If you were part of the beta and want to receive the latest regular updates, please opt out of the beta program by going to the app page on Google Play and scrolling all the way down.
